Title: A reconfigured Kennedy pathway which promotes efficient accumulation of medium-chain fatty acids in leaf oils

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter with duplicated enhancer region (constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use as an sn-1 acyltransferase component in stacked leaf- or seed-oil engineering with MCFA-producing thioesterases, LPAAT, and a substrate-appropriate DGAT1. Best deployed as part of a complete acyl-CoA-dependent TAG pathway rather than alone. Likely useful to raise MCFA incorporation into TAG, but requires downstream sequestration capacity to avoid membrane accumulation and chlorosis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates glycerol-3-phosphate at the sn-1 position using acyl-CoA substrates to form lysophosphatidic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CnGPAT9 is a functional GPAT9 that increases TAG accumulation in transient leaf assays and contributes to incorporation of MCFA acyl-CoAs into glycerolipid assembly, improving MCFA-rich TAG formation when combined with downstream acyltransferases.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Full-length cloning from coconut transcriptome; sequence alignment/phylogeny; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; TAG quantification by TLC/GC-FID; lipid-species profiling by QQQ-LC-MS.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the first committed acylated glycerol backbone for TAG assembly and helps incorporate MCFA into the TAG precursor pool.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient leaf expression in N. benthamiana for 5 days; effects interpreted in the context of coexpression with UcTE, CcTE or CnTE2 and AtWRI1.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous transient expression in infiltrated leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from developing coconut endosperm transcriptome.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CnLPAAT; EgDGAT1; UcTE; CcTE; CnTE2; AtWRI1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome assembly from developing coconut endosperm; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CnGPAT9 appeared to use available acyl-CoAs including C12:0, C14:0 and C16:0 rather than showing a strict MCFA specificity. In the absence of a suitable DGAT1, MCFA-containing intermediates were redirected toward PC rather than efficiently into TAG.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: de novo glycerolipid assembly / acyl-CoA-dependent Kennedy pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: By itself or without appropriate downstream enzymes, MCFA incorporation can still be associated with accumulation in membrane lipids and chlorotic phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Contributory step; not established as the main bottleneck compared with DGAT1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A coconut GPAT9 homolog was identified from endosperm transcriptome and validated as a functional GPAT9 in transient tobacco leaves. 2. Its expression significantly increased TAG relative to p19 control. 3. In reconstructed MCFA pathways, CnGPAT9 helped channel C12:0, C14:0 and C16:0 acyl-CoAs into glycerolipids, but efficient MCFA-rich TAG accumulation required downstream CnLPAAT and especially EgDGAT1.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf oil bodies / leaf TAG fraction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial feedstock for biodiesel and oleochemicals.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter with duplicated enhancer region (constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for stacking with MCFA-generating thioesterases and upstream acyltransferases to raise MCFA-rich leaf or seed oil while reducing membrane damage. Selection or engineering of DGAT1 substrate preference is critical; EgDGAT1 favored C12:0 and C16:0 more than C14:0.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to diacylglycerol to form triacylglycerol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: EgDGAT1 was essential to channel MCFA into TAG, increase total fatty acids and TAG, reduce MCFA accumulation in phospholipids/PC, and alleviate chlorosis in leaves expressing MCFA-producing pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression assays in N. benthamiana; comparison with AtDGAT1 and predicted EgDGAT isoforms; GC-FID analysis of TFA and TAG; TLC phospholipid analysis; QQQ-LC-MS profiling of PC/DAG/TAG species; phenotype observation of chlorosis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and diverts flux away from membrane lipids toward TAG.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient leaf expression for 5 days; interpreted under coexpression with CcTE, UcTE, or CnTE2 and with AtWRI1, CnGPAT9, and CnLPAAT.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous transient expression in infiltrated leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: EgDGAT1.1 was reported as expressed in the palm kernel.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CnGPAT9; CnLPAAT; UcTE; CcTE; CnTE2; AtWRI1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: African oil palm transcriptome used to identify DGAT candidates; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Three predicted oil palm DGAT1 candidates were tested, but sequence analysis suggested EgDGAT1.2 and EgDGAT1.3 may be nonfunctional due to loss of conserved motifs. EgDGAT1.1, referred to as EgDGAT1, improved flux to TAG. The paper infers substrate preference stronger for C12:0 and C16:0 than for C14:0.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: final acylation step of acyl-CoA-dependent TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major growth penalty reported in the transient system; rather, EgDGAT1 reduced chlorosis. However, its substrate preference may shift TAG composition away from desired C14:0 toward C16:0.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EgDGAT1 was necessary to efficiently incorporate nascent MCFA into TAG in leaves. 2. Its expression increased total fatty acid and TAG accumulation, reduced MCFA in phospholipids/PC, and alleviated chlorosis compared with AtDGAT1. 3. Lipidomics showed EgDGAT1 shifted flux from PC species such as PC 24:0 to MCFA-rich TAG species including tri-C12:0, confirming its central role in the reconfigured Kennedy pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf oil bodies / leaf TAG fraction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial feedstock for biodiesel and oleochemicals.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter with duplicated enhancer region (constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking partner with MCFA thioesterases, GPAT9, and DGAT1 to improve incorporation of MCFA at the sn-2 position during TAG assembly. Should be deployed with a suitable DGAT1 to prevent MCFA build-up in membrane lipids.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates lysophosphatidic acid at the sn-2 position using acyl-CoA to form phosphatidic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CnLPAAT increased incorporation of MCFA, especially into the sn-2 position, enhancing accumulation of di-MCFA intermediates and supporting higher MCFA-rich TAG levels in stacked constructs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana with thioesterases, CnGPAT9 and EgDGAT1; GC-FID TAG profiling; QQQ-LC-MS analysis of PC and TAG lipid species.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate precursor required for TAG synthesis and increases MCFA loading into glycerol backbone.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient leaf expression for 5 days under UcTE, CcTE or CnTE2 backgrounds with AtWRI1.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous transient expression in infiltrated leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CnGPAT9; EgDGAT1; UcTE; CcTE; CnTE2; AtWRI1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In UcTE background, addition of CnLPAAT increased PC 24:0, indicating efficient esterification of C12:0 at both sn-1 and sn-2. In CcTE background, CnLPAAT increased PC 28:0, indicating improved C14:0 incorporation at sn-2, but this also correlated with more severe chlorosis unless EgDGAT1 was added.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: de novo glycerolipid assembly / phosphatidic acid branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without EgDGAT1, enhanced MCFA incorporation by CnLPAAT promoted accumulation of saturated MCFA in membrane lipids and aggravated chlorosis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supportive control step; not the principal bottleneck-relieving step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CnLPAAT acted as the second acyltransferase in the reconfigured Kennedy pathway to improve MCFA incorporation into glycerolipids. 2. It increased di-MCFA PC species, indicating more efficient sn-2 esterification of C12:0 or C14:0. 3. Its benefit was maximal when combined with EgDGAT1, which prevented the accumulated MCFA-containing intermediates from remaining in membrane lipids.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf oil bodies / leaf TAG fraction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial feedstock for biodiesel and oleochemicals.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter with duplicated enhancer region (constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: De novo fatty acid synthesis / acyl-ACP termination supplying TAG pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Effective enzyme to generate a laurate-rich acyl-CoA pool for tailored MCFA oil engineering. Best stacked with AtWRI1, CnGPAT9, CnLPAAT and especially EgDGAT1 to maximize C12:0-rich TAG and minimize membrane disruption.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes C12:0-acyl-ACP to release free lauric acid from fatty acid synthesis, thereby determining acyl-chain output toward medium-chain products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: UcTE drove strong accumulation of lauric acid in TAG, reaching about 52% C12:0 in the best stacked pathway, and provided the most effective route for C12:0-rich leaf oil production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression/coexpression in N. benthamiana; GC-FID analysis of TAG fatty acid composition and TAG amount; LC-MS analysis of TAG/PC species.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product by producing the MCFA substrate used for TAG assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient leaf expression for 5 days with or without the high-oil background AtWRI1 + CnLPAAT + EgDGAT1.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous transient expression in infiltrated leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UcTE alone in high-oil background gave TAG with ~48% C12:0; the highest reported C12:0 level (51.6 ± 2.0%) occurred with UcTE + AtWRI1 + CnGPAT9 + CnLPAAT + EgDGAT1. Lipidomics showed a shift from PC 24:0 to TAG 36:0 after EgDGAT1 addition.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: MCFA production module feeding acyl-CoA-dependent TAG assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without a suitable downstream DGAT, laurate can accumulate in membrane lipids and contribute to chlorosis/cell damage.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Lauric acid (C12:0), subsequently converted to C12:0-CoA for glycerolipid assembly
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of fatty acid chain-length output
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UcTE generated a strong C12:0 precursor pool in leaves. 2. In combination with the reconfigured Kennedy pathway, it enabled the highest laurate accumulation in TAG (~51.6%). 3. Efficient capture of UcTE-derived laurate into TAG required downstream CnGPAT9, CnLPAAT and particularly EgDGAT1 to avoid sequestration in PC and chlorosis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf oil bodies / leaf TAG fraction

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter with duplicated enhancer region (constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: De novo fatty acid synthesis / acyl-ACP termination supplying TAG pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to engineer myristate-enriched leaf oils. Works best with CnGPAT9 and CnLPAAT, while a more C14:0-specific DGAT than EgDGAT1 may further improve output and reduce undesired shift toward C16:0.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes medium-chain acyl-ACP, predominantly 14:0-ACP, to release myristic acid from fatty acid synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CcTE produced strong C14:0 accumulation in TAG, reaching about 40% C14:0, but also caused chlorosis unless accompanied by an appropriate downstream DGAT such as EgDGAT1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression/coexpression in N. benthamiana; GC-FID TAG profile and amount; phospholipid analysis by TLC/GC-FID; LC-MS lipid-species profiling; visible chlorosis phenotyping.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product by producing C14:0 used for TAG assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient leaf expression for 5 days; effects evaluated with AtWRI1, CnGPAT9, CnLPAAT, and EgDGAT1.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous transient expression in infiltrated leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CcTE promoted accumulation of PC 28:0 and TAG 42:0 in certain combinations. EgDGAT1 reduced MCFA retention in PC and chlorosis but shifted some flux from C14:0 toward C16:0, suggesting EgDGAT1 is not ideal for explicit C14:0 specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: MCFA production module feeding acyl-CoA-dependent TAG assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong chlorosis and membrane saturation occurred when C14:0 accumulated in membrane lipids; EgDGAT1 only partially matched desired C14:0 specificity and reduced C14:0 proportion while increasing C16:0.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Myristic acid (C14:0), subsequently converted to C14:0-CoA for glycerolipid assembly
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of fatty acid chain-length output
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcTE generated a strong C14:0 precursor pool and enabled approximately 40% myristate accumulation in TAG. 2. CnGPAT9 and CnLPAAT improved incorporation of C14:0 into glycerolipids, but this also increased membrane-lipid saturation and chlorosis. 3. EgDGAT1 redirected C14:0-containing intermediates from PC to TAG and improved phenotype, although it also favored incorporation of some C16:0.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf oil bodies / leaf TAG fraction

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter with duplicated enhancer region (constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: De novo fatty acid synthesis / acyl-ACP termination supplying TAG pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to enrich palmitate/C16:0 in engineered leaf TAG. Native leaf acyltransferases handled this substrate better than for C12:0/C14:0, so added pathway engineering may be less beneficial for composition but still helpful for phenotype stabilization.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes acyl-ACP, predominantly yielding C16:0, to terminate plastid fatty acid synthesis and supply palmitate for downstream TAG assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CnTE2 increased C16:0-rich TAG species, but sequential addition of CnLPAAT and EgDGAT1 did not significantly improve total TAG or TAG composition, indicating native acyltransferases already utilize increased C16:0 relatively efficiently.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression/coexpression in N. benthamiana; GC-FID analysis of TAG profile and amount; LC-MS profiling of TAG and PC species; chlorosis assessment.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product by producing C16:0 used for TAG assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient leaf expression for 5 days with or without high-oil background and with sequential acyltransferase additions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous transient expression in infiltrated leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CnTE2 expression alone produced TAG with ~58% C16:0 in the high-oil background. Although extra acyltransferases did not markedly improve TAG composition, EgDGAT1 reduced MCFA accumulation in PC and partially reduced chlorosis.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: MCFA/longer-chain saturated fatty acid production module feeding TAG assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Chlorosis still occurred, though less severely after EgDGAT1 addition; benefit of extra acyltransferases was limited for bulk TAG composition.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Palmitic acid (C16:0), subsequently converted to C16:0-CoA for glycerolipid assembly
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of fatty acid chain-length output
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CnTE2 drove strong C16:0 accumulation in TAG, reaching about 58% when expressed alone in the high-oil background. 2. Native acyltransferases in N. benthamiana appeared comparatively able to use the extra C16:0, so added CnLPAAT/EgDGAT1 caused limited gains in TAG amount or composition. 3. EgDGAT1 still lowered PC accumulation and partially improved chlorosis, indicating some benefit for membrane homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf oil bodies / leaf TAG fraction
